span.p:
Where PR #7662 Fits in Karmada
An execution orchestrator between intent and the existing scheduling pipeline

Choose when, workload,
from / to, frequency
OUT OF SCOPE

Binding Controller
materialize per-cluster desired state

span.p:
Work Status + RB/CRB Status Controllers
member status -> Work.status ->
Binding.status.aggregatedStatus

div.style: color: rgb(127, 29, 29) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
UNRESOLVED CONTRACT
WR executors and Scheduler may both write Binding.spec.clusters.
The durable target-first encoding and single-writer rule are not defined.

div.style: color: rgb(23, 37, 84) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
COMPONENT POSITION
#7662 does not replace the Scheduler, Binding Controller, Work, or execution path.
It proposes a long-running orchestration layer that drives and observes them.

div.style: color: rgb(55, 65, 81) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Legend: blue = current Karmada | green = proposed #7662 | blue/green = existing controller expanded | yellow = shared API state | gray = out of scope | red dashed = unresolved
